title.zh: 看不見的界線如何左右選票？德國研究揭開行政邊界對投票行為的隱形影響
title.en: The Invisible Lines That Divide Voters: How Administrative Borders Shape Electoral Behavior in Germany

summary.zh:
當我們討論選舉地理學時，往往聚焦於行政區劃內的投票結果，但這篇發表在《政治地理學》的研究卻翻轉了視角。德國學者Jona-Frederik Baumert提出創新方法，不再分析單一選區的投票數據，而是測量相鄰行政單位之間的『選舉差異度』，就像用顯微鏡觀察政治版圖的斷層線。

研究團隊開發出類似加拉格爾指數的改良標準差算法，量化不同層級行政邊界（如州界、縣市界）對投票行為的影響。結果顯示，前東西德邊界至今仍是德國最顯著的政治斷層，而選區劃分對政黨票幾乎沒有影響，卻顯著左右候選人得票。這項發現挑戰了傳統選舉地理學的分析框架，為理解行政區劃的政治效應提供全新工具。

更耐人尋味的是，州級邊界造成的政黨票差異程度，甚至超過縣市邊界。這暗示選民的政治認同可能與『想像的共同體』尺度密切相關——人們對『邦國』層級的政治歸屬感，比地方政府劃分更能驅動投票選擇。該研究為解構區域主義與行政區劃改革提供了實證基礎，下一站或許該問：這些隱形界線在其他多層治理國家（如台灣或美國）會呈現怎樣的政治地貌？
summary.en:
Political scientists have long studied voting patterns within electoral districts, but groundbreaking research in *Political Geography* flips this approach on its head. Jona-Frederik Baumert's study introduces a novel methodology that measures electoral differences *between* adjacent territorial units rather than within them—effectively mapping the political fault lines along administrative borders.

Using a modified standard deviation metric akin to the Gallagher index, the study quantifies how different types of boundaries (state, county, municipal) influence voting behavior in Germany. The findings reveal that the former Inner-German border remains the most potent political divide decades after reunification. While constituency boundaries significantly affect candidate votes, they show negligible impact on party votes—a distinction that challenges conventional electoral geography frameworks.

The research uncovers a hierarchy of border effects: State boundaries create sharper electoral divides than county lines in party voting patterns. This suggests voters' political identities are more strongly tied to larger-scale 'imagined communities' than local administrative units. The methodology offers a powerful new tool for analyzing how multi-level governance structures—from federal systems to autonomous regions—shape democratic representation. As nations worldwide grapple with electoral reform and regionalism, this German case study provides a template for decoding the hidden geography of political allegiance.

keyArticle.title: Which border matters most? An analysis of how different types of internal boundaries affect voting behavior
